title: Fix CSV/Excel parser to extract gateway details and packet stats from uploaded file format

requirements.summary: Update csvParser.ts to detect and skip metadata rows 1–7, correctly identify row 8 as the header row, and parse data rows starting from row 9
acceptanceCriteria: Uploading the Excel file no longer shows 'No data rows found' error
Parser correctly skips metadata rows 1–7 (Customer Name, Group, Gateway, Start Date, End Date) and identifies row 8 as the column header row
Data rows starting from row 9 are successfully parsed

file_operations.description: Modify the parseFile function to detect and skip the metadata header block (rows 1–7) before identifying the actual data header row (row 8). Implement logic to recognize the metadata block by detecting 'Customer Name :' or 'Gateway :' patterns in the first few rows, then scan for the first row containing column headers like 'Date', 'Timezone', 'PktState', or 'GPS Status'. Store the detected header row index and begin data parsing from the next row. Update error handling to avoid throwing 'No data rows found' when metadata rows are present.

requirements.summary: Extract gateway details (unit name, start date, end date) from metadata rows and make them available for auto-population
acceptanceCriteria: Unit name is extracted from the 'Gateway :' metadata row and populated in the import form or report entry
Start date is extracted from the 'Start Date & Time :' metadata row
End date (current date) is extracted from the 'End Date & Time :' metadata row
Extracted values are shown to the user before submission (e.g. in the parsed preview)

file_operations.description: Add a metadata extraction function that parses rows 1–7 to extract gateway details. For each metadata row, split by the colon separator and trim whitespace to get the key-value pair. Extract the Gateway name from the 'Gateway :' row (e.g., 'N13-5_D20_Lite'), parse the start date from 'Start Date & Time :' row (e.g., '8 Feb'26 12:00 AM'), and parse the end date from 'End Date & Time :' row (e.g., '23 Feb'26 12:00 AM'). Return these extracted metadata fields alongside the parsed data rows in the ParsedFileResult structure. Update the ParsedFileResult type to include optional metadata fields: unitName, startDate, endDate.

Update the file parsing handler to capture extracted metadata (unitName, startDate, endDate) from the parser result. Store this metadata in component state alongside the parsed rows. If metadata is present, display it in the parsed file preview card before the data table. Add UI elements to show the extracted unit name, start date, and end date to the user before they submit the import.

requirements.summary: Compute total packet count and stored packet count from the 'PktState' column and map them to ParsedRow output
acceptanceCriteria: Parser finds the 'PktState' column by header name (not hardcoded index) in the header row
Rows with PktState value 'Normal' are counted as total/normal packets
Rows with PktState value 'Stored' are counted as stored packets
Both counts are correctly reflected in the imported report entry

file_operations.description: After identifying the header row, locate the 'PktState' column by scanning the header row for a case-insensitive match of 'PktState' (trim whitespace). Store the column index. For each data row, read the value at the PktState column index. Count rows where PktState equals 'Normal' (case-insensitive, trimmed) as total/normal packets, and count rows where PktState equals 'Stored' (case-insensitive, trimmed) as stored packets. Aggregate these counts across all rows in the file and include them in the ParsedRow or ParsedFileResult output. Map totalPackets to the 'Normal' count and storedPackets to the 'Stored' count.

requirements.summary: Compute GPS valid fix count from the 'GPS Status' column and map it to ParsedRow output
acceptanceCriteria: Parser finds the 'GPS Status' column by header name in the header row
Rows where GPS Status equals 'Valid' (case-insensitive) are counted as GPS valid fix packets
Rows where GPS Status is 'Invalid' or other values are not counted as valid GPS fixes
The valid GPS fix count is correctly reflected in the imported report entry

file_operations.description: After identifying the header row, locate the 'GPS Status' column by scanning the header row for a case-insensitive match of 'GPS Status' (trim whitespace). Store the column index. For each data row, read the value at the GPS Status column index. Count rows where GPS Status equals 'Valid' (case-insensitive, trimmed) as GPS valid fix packets. Aggregate this count across all rows in the file and include it in the ParsedRow or ParsedFileResult output as validGpsPackets. Do not count rows where GPS Status is 'Invalid' or any other value.

requirements.summary: Display a summary preview showing extracted gateway details and computed statistics before user submits the import
acceptanceCriteria: After a successful file parse, a preview card is shown with unit name, start date, end date, total packets, stored packets, and GPS valid fix count
The preview appears before the user clicks submit/import
If parsing fails or any key field is missing, a clear error message is shown instead

file_operations.description: After a file is successfully parsed, render a preview card that displays the extracted metadata (unit name, start date, end date) and computed statistics (total packet count, stored packet count, GPS valid fix count) before the existing parsed rows table. Use a Card component with a clear heading like 'Parsed File Summary' or 'Import Preview'. Display each field with a label (e.g., 'Unit Name:', 'Start Date:', 'End Date:', 'Total Packets:', 'Stored Packets:', 'GPS Valid Fix Count:'). If any of these fields are missing or undefined, show a warning message indicating incomplete data. Position this preview above the submit/import button so users can review before submission.
